codeReview.summary: The diff stays close to the intended merge-blocker cleanup and the R2 waiver rewrite is appropriately narrow, but the feature still misses a blocking cross-platform path issue: it updates `docs/architecture.md` while the repo's canonical docs contract, links, and lint script all require `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md`. Local validation passed only because this workspace is on a case-insensitive macOS filesystem; on Linux CI, the canonical file path still does not exist, so merge-readiness is not actually restored.

issues.description: This feature edits the lowercase compatibility file instead of making the canonical `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md` path real. `git ls-files "docs/*[Aa]rchitecture.md"` shows only `docs/architecture.md`, while `ci/lint-knowledgebase.sh` requires `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md` and multiple canonical links point to that uppercase path. On a case-sensitive Linux runner, PR #178 can still fail knowledgebase lint even though it passed locally on macOS.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The feature's verification contract required `bash scripts/enforce_codex_review_policy.sh`, but `.factory/services.yaml` has docs validation bundles that omit this command, so the worker had to run it ad hoc.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Feature verification steps in mission `features.json` include `bash scripts/enforce_codex_review_policy.sh`; `.factory/services.yaml:58-61` defines `docs-governance` and `docs-contract-guards` without that command.

summary: Reviewed the handoff, transcript skeleton, skill instructions, and commit `61eef17fd722480c6d9d48a4daaae975d8204969`. The docs/R2 changes are narrowly scoped, but the feature still fails merge-readiness because it relies on macOS case-insensitive filesystem behavior and does not satisfy the canonical `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md` path required by the repo's lint contract.
